_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Overlap details popup (read-only)

steps.description: Add GET /project/{slug}/batch/{batch_id}/overlap/{held_item_id} to dashboard/routers/batches.py. The endpoint queries DaemonEvent rows where event_type='item_held_for_scope' AND entity_id=held_item_id AND project_id resolved from slug AND created_at within the same 300s window used by _get_scope_statuses; for each unique blocking_item_id (preserving most-recent-wins on duplicates) it joins WorkItem to fetch the title; groups payloads by blocking_item_id; renders dashboard/templates/fragments/batch_overlap_modal.html with the grouped data. On no matching event: 404 with a small fragment 'No overlap details available — the item may have been released since this page rendered.' Add a pure helper `group_overlap_events(events) -> list[tuple[str, list[str]]]` (blocking_item_id, conflicting_globs) co-located in batches.py and importable from tests/unit/; the route fetches each blocking item's title separately via WorkItem. Read-only — no DB writes, no SELECT FOR UPDATE.

Per-agent review of S01: endpoint contract matches the design (URL, params, 200/404 semantics, read-only); query window matches _get_scope_statuses (300s); the helper is pure and importable for unit testing; Jinja context auto-escapes every globbed path string (a glob like `dashboard/templates/**` must NOT break HTML); no Tailwind classes are introduced that require make css; the response is a fragment (no base.html); 404 returns valid HTML, not an HTTP exception page.
Make the Held pill clickable in dashboard/templates/fragments/batch_items_rows.html: convert the `` wrapping pill_text into a `

Per-agent review of S03: the batch_items_rows.html trigger calls the modal endpoint via hx-target; the modal partial is the single source of truth (no template duplication); queue.html is unchanged (out of scope); CSS additions are appended (not replacing) and use the `iw-modal-*` prefix to avoid colliding with existing classes; Esc handler is scoped (no global document.body keydown leak); no `<form>`, `<input>`, no POST endpoints introduced anywhere — this is read-only display.

Two new test modules. (1) tests/unit/test_batch_overlap_grouping.py — exercise group_overlap_events with: empty list, single event, multiple events to same blocking_item_id (most recent wins, older payload dropped), events to multiple blocking items (insertion order preserved), event missing event_metadata key (graceful skip). (2) tests/dashboard/test_batch_overlap_modal.py — TestClient + db_session testcontainer fixture, seed a Project + WorkItem (held + blocking) + Batch + BatchItem + two DaemonEvent rows of type item_held_for_scope, hit the new endpoint, assert: 200 + Content-Type starts with 'text/html'; response body contains BOTH blocking_item_ids; response body contains EVERY glob from the seeded payloads (use exact string contains); response body contains links to /project/<slug>/item/<blocking_item_id>; response is a fragment (asserts NOT contains '<html' and NOT contains '<body'). Also: 404 case when there is no recent event — assert status code is 404 and the fragment contains 'No overlap details available'. Demonstrate each new test fails before the implementation by capturing tdd_red_evidence (run targeted file with deliberate broken assertion / missing route).

Per-agent review of S05: assertion strength — uses substring-contains on full glob strings (NOT vacuous .ok or count==N tests); both error paths exercised (200 and 404); the unit test for grouping is pure (no DB); the dashboard test uses the testcontainer db_session and does NOT touch the live DB; tdd_red_evidence is captured for both new modules and shows an AssertionError or 404-where-200-expected style failure, not a collection error.

Global cross-agent review of S01..S06: every AC (AC1-AC6) verified; scope discipline — git diff origin/main -- orch/ executor/ MUST be empty (no daemon or CLI changes); orch/db/migrations/ MUST be empty (no schema change); the modal partial is a single fragment file (structured for CR-00078 reuse); the read-only contract holds (no POST endpoints, no DB writes); the Esc handler is properly scoped; CSS uses the iw-modal-* prefix and is appended (no Tailwind recompile required per CLAUDE.md).
QV: Assertion scanner (forbid new vacuous tests)

QV: Browser verification — open the batch detail page for a live Held item (BATCH-00127 if available in seed; else any held item visible in the e2e stack), click the Held pill, assert modal opens with at least one grouped section and a Close control, click Close, assert modal disappears.

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill. Note whether the modal partial is a single clean fragment ready for CR-00078 reuse, whether tests caught the +N truncation gap, whether the 404 path was exercised by browser_verification or only by integration tests, and the impact (if any) on CR-00078's planned modifications to the same partial.
